title: Brainstorm: First Settlement
description: Sling idea agents to brainstorm the first settlement in this new world. What is this place, what do people do here, what makes it interesting?
closeReason: Brainstormed first settlement: Haul's Rest, a river-ford waystation turned permanent town. Local lens pitch sent. Haulers' Guild vs. settler conflict, mundane economy (farrier/bonesetter/harness repair), no cliches.

title: Create Caltworks settlement file
description: Write vault/Compendium/World Atlas/Settlements/Caltworks.md. Salt-rendering town that grew from seasonal camp. Calt family controls evaporation flats by squatter's right. Newer arrivals resent salt tithe. Most powerful person: woman who decides whose salt gets weighed first. White-crusted streets, brine/woodsmoke smell. No walls, no defensible position.
closeReason: Created Caltworks.md: salt-rendering settlement with Calt family tithe conflict, Harla Calt as weighing yard gatekeeper, degrading flats as hidden GM pressure.

title: Create Caltworks NPCs and registry
description: Write 2-3 NPC files in vault/Compendium/NPCs/ and update vault/NPCRegistry.md. Key NPCs: (1) The salt weigher, the de facto most powerful person in town. (2) A Calt family head who claims ownership of the flats. (3) A newcomer hauler who represents the resentment. Ground these in the salt economy.
closeReason: Created Maren Seedeck, Oran Calt, Dessa Vael NPC files and updated NPCRegistry.md. Three NPCs grounded in Caltworks salt economy with distinct motivations and interconnected tensions.

title: Fix NPC references in Caltworks.md
description: Replace Harla Calt references with Maren Seedeck, replace Pem Varsk references with Dessa Vael, and add Oran Calt as notable resident. The worldwriter and charwriter used different names; the charwriter names are canonical.
closeReason: Fixed NPC references in Caltworks.md: Harla Calt -> Maren Seedeck, Pem Varsk -> Dessa Vael, added Oran Calt. Updated GM secrets to match canonical charwriter files.

title: Update CHANGELOG, Timeline, and World Overview
description: Update vault/CHANGELOG.md with what was built (Caltworks settlement, 3 NPCs). Update vault/Timeline.md with Caltworks founding events. Update vault/World Overview.md with emerging themes: salt economy, labor tension, frontier settlement growing beyond its informal governance.
closeReason: Updated CHANGELOG, Timeline, and World Overview with Caltworks founding, 3 NPCs, timeline from founding to present, and emerging world themes.

title: Brainstorm: Salt buyer settlement
description: Who buys the salt from Caltworks? What settlement is at the other end of the road? What do they produce that Caltworks needs?
closeReason: Pitched Harrow Fell: a tannery town that buys salt for hide-curing, sends timber back west as return cargo, creating a mutual dependency with Caltworks neither settlement discusses openly.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (pragmatist)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? Where does it go? What does the trade route look like? Use the PRAGMATIST lens.
closeReason: Pragmatist brainstorm complete: identified fishing settlements as dominant buyer, 3-leg cart-based trade route, merchant factors as price-extracting middlemen, and the tiered weigh fee / hauler stoppage offers as buyer-vs-buyer competition using Caltworks tensions as leverage. Shrinking flats represent undisclosed supply risk to buyers.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (weird)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? What unexpected angle exists? Use the WEIRD lens.
closeReason: Brainstorm complete. 5 weird-lens ideas generated for 'who buys Caltworks salt': (1) buyer wants mineral byproduct not salt, (2) buyer is a land acquisition scout exploiting known flat degradation, (3) buyer is someone workers would refuse to supply, (4) shipment volume fraud in the weighing yard, (5) generational tithe obligation to an unknown recipient. Best gameplay angles: land acquisition (ticking clock antagonist), wrong commodity (investigation hook), unconscionable buyer (moral conflict). Results mailed to coordinator.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (local)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? What do locals think about the buyers? Use the LOCAL lens.
closeReason: Brainstormed who buys Caltworks salt from the local perspective. Identified merchant factors as primary visible buyer type, worker attitudes toward buyers, tensions around the Dessa factor offer and Calt exclusive buyer relationships, and what locals don't know about buyer-side competition and flat degradation.

title: Brainstorm: Who buys the salt? (player)
description: Brainstorm idea: Who buys Caltworks salt? What makes this gameable? Use the PLAYER lens.
closeReason: Brainstormed 7 salt buyer types from player lens: smoker/curer, wholesale factor, garrison quartermaster, estate steward, road merchant, competing supplier's agent, and tanner. Identified cross-cutting player dynamics around information asymmetry (degrading flats), timing plays (factor stoppage scheme), and buyer choices as implicit faction support. Anti-cliche check applied throughout.

title: Lore check: salt trade direction
description: Verify that establishing a fishing settlement as primary salt buyer, a waypoint staging yard, and a land-acquisition merchant factor does not contradict existing vault content or hit anti-cliche rules.
closeReason: Lore check complete. All three proposed elements (fishing settlement as salt buyer, waypoint staging yard, land-acquisition merchant factor) are clear of contradictions and anti-cliche violations. One coherence note: clarify whether the land-acquisition factor is the same unnamed merchant factor already in vault (Caltworks.md:74, Dessa Vael.md:53) or a new distinct actor.

title: Create fishing settlement (salt buyer)
description: Create the fishing/curing settlement that is Caltworks' primary salt buyer. See dispatch mail for full details.
closeReason: Created Rackby settlement file: fishing/curing town on Greymere lake, Caltworks' primary salt buyer via merchant factor Vess Cordham. Full format match with Caltworks. New NPCs named for charwriter follow-up: Vess Cordham, Tamma Wreich, Burl.

title: Create waypoint staging yard
description: Create the waypoint/staging yard between Caltworks and the fishing settlement. See dispatch mail for full details.
closeReason: Created The Fork waypoint staging yard between Caltworks and the fishing coast. File: vault/Compendium/World Atlas/Settlements/The Fork.md. Updated NPCRegistry and CHANGELOG.

title: Create merchant factor NPC
description: Create the merchant factor NPC who is behind the land acquisition play. See dispatch mail for full details.
